<!-- BEGIN:nextjs-agent-rules -->
# This is NOT the Next.js you know## Measure Workflow

## Measure Workflow

All development runs through the **Measure** spec-driven development framework exclusively. At the start of every session:

1. Load the `measure` skill
2. Read `measure/index.md` to understand the project context
3. Follow the workflow defined in `measure/workflow.md`

Key reference files:
- `measure/tracks.md` — Active work registry
- `measure/tracks/<track_id>/plan.md` — Task checklist
- `measure/product.md` — Product vision
- `measure/tech-stack.md` — Technology choices
- `measure/lessons-learned.md` — Project memory
- `measure/tech-debt.md` — Known shortcuts

Never start significant work without an active track. Always update `measure/tracks.md` and the current track's `plan.md` before and after work.

<content>
1: import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server';
2: import { getAllRuns } from '@/lib/runs';
3: import { filterRuns, type QueryFilters } from '@/lib/export';
4: import { validateAuth, authResponse } from '@/lib/api-auth';
5: 
6: export async function GET(request: NextRequest) {
7:   const authResult = await validateAuth(request);
8:   if (!authResult.allowed && authResult.status) {
9:     return authResponse(authResult.error!, authResult.status);
10:   }
11: 
12:   try {
13:     const { searchParams } = new URL(request.url);
14: 
15:     const filters: QueryFilters = {};
16:     if (searchParams.has('model')) filters.model = searchParams.get('model')!;
17:     if (searchParams.has('task')) filters.task = searchParams.get('task')!;
18:     if (searchParams.has('startDate')) filters.startDate = searchParams.get('startDate')!;
19:     if (searchParams.has('endDate')) filters.endDate = searchParams.get('endDate')!;
20:     if (searchParams.has('minScore')) filters.minScore = parseFloat(searchParams.get('minScore')!);
21:     if (searchParams.has('maxScore')) filters.maxScore = parseFloat(searchParams.get('maxScore')!);
22: 
23:     const limit = searchParams.has('limit') ? parseInt(searchParams.get('limit')!, 10) : 100;
24:     const offset = searchParams.has('offset') ? parseInt(searchParams.get('offset')!, 10) : 0;
25: 
26:     const runs = await getAllRuns();
27:     const filtered = filterRuns(runs, filters);
28:     const total = filtered.length;
29:     const paginated = filtered.slice(offset, offset + limit);
30: 
31:     return NextResponse.json({
32:       count: paginated.length,
33:       total,
34:       limit,
35:       offset,
36:       runs: paginated,
37:     });
38:   } catch (error) {
39:     return NextResponse.json(
40:       { error: 'Failed to fetch runs', details: String(error) },
41:       { status: 500 }
42:     );
43:   }
44: }

(End of file - total 44 lines)
</content>

type RunDetail = { id: string; model: string; harness: string; harness_version: string; benchmark_version: string; run_date: string; wall_time_seconds: number; total_score: number; scores: { functional_correctness: number; integration_quality: number; regression_safety: number; minimality: number; process_quality: number; }; test_results: { suite: string; name: string; status: "pass" | "fail" | "skip"; duration_ms?: number | undefined; error_message?: string | undefined; }[]; artifacts: { name: string; type: "log" | "screenshot" | "report" | "other"; url: string; size_bytes?: number | undefined; }[]; provider?: string | undefined; dataset_version?: string | undefined; task_id?: string | undefined; task_domain?: string | undefined; diff?: string | undefined; track?: "fixed" | "native" | undefined; }

<path>/tmp/ralb-manual-benchmark-xiaomi-mimo-v25-pro/worktree/src/lib/export.ts</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: import type { RunDetail } from './schemas';
2: 
3: export interface QueryFilters {
4:   model?: string;
5:   task?: string;
6:   startDate?: string;
7:   endDate?: string;
8:   minScore?: number;
9:   maxScore?: number;
10: }
11: 
12: export interface SummaryStats {
13:   mean: number;
14:   median: number;
15:   p95: number;
16: }
17: 
18: export function filterRuns(runs: RunDetail[], filters: QueryFilters): RunDetail[] {
19:   return runs.filter(run => {
20:     if (filters.model && run.model !== filters.model) {
21:       return false;
22:     }
23:     if (filters.task && run.task_id !== filters.task) {
24:       return false;
25:     }
26:     if (filters.startDate && run.run_date < filters.startDate) {
27:       return false;
28:     }
29:     if (filters.endDate && run.run_date > filters.endDate) {
30:       return false;
31:     }
32:     if (filters.minScore !== undefined && run.total_score < filters.minScore) {
33:       return false;
34:     }
35:     if (filters.maxScore !== undefined && run.total_score > filters.maxScore) {
36:       return false;
37:     }
38:     return true;
39:   });
40: }
41: 
42: export function computeStats(scores: number[]): SummaryStats {
43:   if (scores.length === 0) {
44:     return { mean: 0, median: 0, p95: 0 };
45:   }
46: 
47:   const sorted = [...scores].sort((a, b) => a - b);
48:   const sum = sorted.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0);
49:   const mean = sum / sorted.length;
50: 
51:   const mid = Math.floor(sorted.length / 2);
52:   const median = sorted.length % 2 !== 0
53:     ? sorted[mid]
54:     : (sorted[mid - 1] + sorted[mid]) / 2;
55: 
56:   const p95Index = Math.ceil(sorted.length * 0.95) - 1;
57:   const p95 = sorted[p95Index];
58: 
59:   return { mean, median, p95 };
60: }
61: 
62: export function exportToCSV(runs: RunDetail[]): string {
63:   const headers = ['id', 'model', 'run_date', 'total_score', 'task_id', 'harness', 'wall_time_seconds'];
64:   const lines: string[] = [headers.join(',')];
65: 
66:   for (const run of runs) {
67:     const row = [
68:       escapeCSV(run.id),
69:       escapeCSV(run.model),
70:       escapeCSV(run.run_date),
71:       run.total_score.toString(),
72:       escapeCSV(run.task_id || ''),
73:       escapeCSV(run.harness),
74:       run.wall_time_seconds.toString(),
75:     ];
76:     lines.push(row.join(','));
77:   }
78: 
79:   return lines.join('\n');
80: }
81: 
82: function escapeCSV(value: string): string {
83:   if (value.includes(',') || value.includes('"') || value.includes('\n')) {
84:     return `"${value.replace(/"/g, '""')}"`;
85:   }
86:   return value;
87: }
88: 
89: export function runsToLeaderboardFormat(runs: RunDetail[]) {
90:   const byModel = new Map<string, RunDetail[]>();
91:   for (const run of runs) {
92:     const existing = byModel.get(run.model) || [];
93:     existing.push(run);
94:     byModel.set(run.model, existing);
95:   }
96: 
97:   return Array.from(byModel.entries()).map(([model, modelRuns]) => {
98:     const scores = modelRuns.map(r => r.total_score);
99:     const stats = computeStats(scores);
100:     return {
101:       model,
102:       runs: modelRuns.length,
103:       latestRunDate: modelRuns[0].run_date,
104:       meanScore: stats.mean,
105:       medianScore: stats.median,
106:       p95Score: stats.p95,
107:     };
108:   });
109: }

(End of file - total 109 lines)
</content>

type Task = { id: string; title: string; difficulty: string; description: string; version: string; status: "draft" | "review" | "published"; domain?: string | undefined; repo_context?: string | undefined; acceptance_criteria?: string[] | undefined; rubric?: string[] | undefined; structured_rubric?: { label: string; weight: number; description: string; }[] | undefined; }

type Run = { id: string; model: string; harness: string; benchmark_version: string; score: number; provider?: string | undefined; dataset_version?: string | undefined; task_id?: string | undefined; subscores?: Record<string, number> | undefined; date?: string | undefined; }

type LeaderboardEntry = { model: string; score: number; provider?: string | undefined; harness?: string | undefined; subscores?: Record<string, number> | undefined; date?: string | undefined; dataset_version?: string | undefined; track?: "fixed" | "native" | undefined; }

type DatasetVersion = { version: string; created_at: string; description: string; tasks: string[]; }

type TaskVersion = { version: string; created_at: string; task_id: string; task_data: { id: string; title: string; difficulty: string; description: string; version: string; status: "draft" | "review" | "published"; domain?: string | undefined; repo_context?: string | undefined; acceptance_criteria?: string[] | undefined; rubric?: string[] | undefined; structured_rubric?: { label: string; weight: number; description: string; }[] | undefined; }; change_summary?: string | undefined; }

type TaskTemplate = { id: string; name: string; description: string; difficulty: "easy" | "medium" | "hard"; domain: string; structured_rubric: { label: string; weight: number; description: string; }[]; example_acceptance_criteria: string[]; }

<path>/tmp/ralb-manual-benchmark-xiaomi-mimo-v25-pro/worktree/src/lib/data.ts</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: import fs from 'fs/promises';
2: import path from 'path';
3: import { Task, TaskSchema, Run, RunSchema, LeaderboardEntry, LeaderboardSchema, DatasetVersion, DatasetVersionSchema, TaskVersion, TaskVersionSchema, TaskTemplate, TaskTemplatesSchema } from './schemas';
4: import { z } from 'zod';
5: 
6: const DATA_DIR = path.join(process.cwd(), 'data');
7: 
8: async function readJsonDirectory<T>(dirName: string, schema: z.ZodSchema<T>): Promise<T[]> {
9:   try {
10:     const dirPath = path.join(DATA_DIR, dirName);
11:     const files = await fs.readdir(dirPath);
12:     
13:     const validItems: T[] = [];
14: 
15:     for (const file of files) {
16:       if (file.endsWith('.json')) {
17:         const filePath = path.join(dirPath, file);
18:         const content = await fs.readFile(filePath, 'utf-8');
19:         const parsed = JSON.parse(content);
20:         
21:         // Handle both single objects and arrays of objects
22:         const items = Array.isArray(parsed) ? parsed : (parsed.entries ? parsed.entries : [parsed]);
23: 
24:         for (const item of items) {
25:           const result = schema.safeParse(item);
26:           if (result.success) {
27:             validItems.push(result.data);
28:           }
29:         }
30:       }
31:     }
32:     
33:     return validItems;
34:   } catch {
35:     return [];
36:   }
37: }
38: 
39: export async function getTasks(): Promise<Task[]> {
40:   return readJsonDirectory('tasks', TaskSchema);
41: }
42: 
43: export async function getTaskById(id: string): Promise<Task | undefined> {
44:   const tasks = await getTasks();
45:   return tasks.find(t => t.id === id);
46: }
47: 
48: export async function getRuns(): Promise<Run[]> {
49:   return readJsonDirectory('runs', RunSchema);
50: }
51: 
52: export async function getRunById(id: string): Promise<Run | undefined> {
53:   const runs = await getRuns();
54:   return runs.find(r => r.id === id);
55: }
56: 
57: export async function getLeaderboard(): Promise<LeaderboardEntry[]> {
58:   return readJsonDirectory('leaderboard', LeaderboardSchema);
59: }
60: 
61: export async function getCurrentDatasetVersion(): Promise<DatasetVersion | null> {
62:   try {
63:     const filePath = path.join(DATA_DIR, 'datasets', 'dataset.json');
64:     const content = await fs.readFile(filePath, 'utf-8');
65:     const parsed = JSON.parse(content);
66:     const result = DatasetVersionSchema.safeParse(parsed);
67:     if (result.success) {
68:       return result.data;
69:     }
70:     return null;
71:   } catch {
72:     return null;
73:   }
74: }
75: 
76: export async function getRunsByVersion(version: string): Promise<Run[]> {
77:   const runs = await getRuns();
78:   return runs.filter(r => r.dataset_version === version);
79: }
80: 
81: export async function getRunCountForTask(taskId: string): Promise<number> {
82:   const runs = await getRuns();
83:   return runs.filter(r => r.task_id === taskId).length;
84: }
85: 
86: export async function getTaskVersions(taskId: string): Promise<TaskVersion[]> {
87:   const versionsDir = path.join(DATA_DIR, 'tasks', 'versions', taskId);
88:   try {
89:     const files = await fs.readdir(versionsDir);
90:     const versions: TaskVersion[] = [];
91: 
92:     for (const file of files) {
93:       if (file.endsWith('.json')) {
94:         const filePath = path.join(versionsDir, file);
95:         const content = await fs.readFile(filePath, 'utf-8');
96:         const parsed = JSON.parse(content);
97:         const result = TaskVersionSchema.safeParse(parsed);
98:         if (result.success) {
99:           versions.push(result.data);
100:         }
101:       }
102:     }
103: 
104:     return versions.sort((a, b) => new Date(b.created_at).getTime() - new Date(a.created_at).getTime());
105:   } catch {
106:     return [];
107:   }
108: }
109: 
110: export async function cleanupTaskVersions(taskId: string, maxVersions: number = 50): Promise<number> {
111:   const versionsDir = path.join(DATA_DIR, 'tasks', 'versions', taskId);
112:   try {
113:     const files = await fs.readdir(versionsDir);
114:     const versionFiles = files.filter(f => f.endsWith('.json'));
115: 
116:     const versions: { filePath: string; created_at: Date }[] = [];
117:     for (const file of versionFiles) {
118:       const filePath = path.join(versionsDir, file);
119:       const content = await fs.readFile(filePath, 'utf-8');
120:       const parsed = JSON.parse(content);
121:       const result = TaskVersionSchema.safeParse(parsed);
122:       if (result.success) {
123:         versions.push({ filePath, created_at: new Date(result.data.created_at) });
124:       }
125:     }
126: 
127:     versions.sort((a, b) => b.created_at.getTime() - a.created_at.getTime());
128: 
129:     const toDelete = versions.slice(maxVersions);
130:     for (const { filePath } of toDelete) {
131:       await fs.unlink(filePath);
132:     }
133: 
134:     return toDelete.length;
135:   } catch {
136:     return 0;
137:   }
138: }
139: 
140: export async function saveTaskVersion(taskId: string, taskData: Task, changeSummary?: string): Promise<TaskVersion> {
141:   const versionsDir = path.join(DATA_DIR, 'tasks', 'versions', taskId);
142:   await fs.mkdir(versionsDir, { recursive: true });
143: 
144:   const version = taskData.version || '1.0';
145:   const timestamp = new Date().toISOString();
146:   const versionId = `${version}_${Date.now()}`;
147: 
148:   const taskVersion: TaskVersion = {
149:     version,
150:     created_at: timestamp,
151:     task_id: taskId,
152:     task_data: taskData,
153:     change_summary: changeSummary,
154:   };
155: 
156:   const filePath = path.join(versionsDir, `${versionId}.json`);
157:   await fs.writeFile(filePath, JSON.stringify(taskVersion, null, 2));
158: 
159:   return taskVersion;
160: }
161: 
162: export async function getTaskTemplates(): Promise<TaskTemplate[]> {
163:   try {
164:     const filePath = path.join(DATA_DIR, 'templates', 'task_templates.json');
165:     const content = await fs.readFile(filePath, 'utf-8');
166:     const parsed = JSON.parse(content);
167:     const result = TaskTemplatesSchema.safeParse(parsed);
168:     if (result.success) {
169:       return result.data.templates;
170:     }
171:     return [];
172:   } catch {
173:     return [];
174:   }
175: }

(End of file - total 175 lines)
</content>

1: import { describe, it, expect } from 'vitest';
2: import type { RunDetail } from '../../src/lib/schemas';
3: import { filterRuns, computeStats, exportToCSV, type QueryFilters } from '../../src/lib/export';
4: 
5: function createMockRun(id: string, model: string, runDate: string, totalScore: number, taskId?: string): RunDetail {
6:   return {
7:     id,
8:     model,
9:     harness: 'opencode',
10:     harness_version: '1.0.0',
11:     benchmark_version: '1.0',
12:     run_date: runDate,
13:     wall_time_seconds: 100,
14:     total_score: totalScore,
15:     scores: {
16:       functional_correctness: Math.round(totalScore * 40),
17:       integration_quality: Math.round(totalScore * 25),
18:       regression_safety: Math.round(totalScore * 20),
19:       minimality: Math.round(totalScore * 10),
20:       process_quality: Math.round(totalScore * 5),
21:     },
22:     test_results: [],
23:     artifacts: [],
24:     task_id: taskId,
25:   };
26: }
27: 
28: describe('Query Filtering', () => {
29:   const runs: RunDetail[] = [
30:     createMockRun('run-1', 'gpt-4o', '2026-04-01', 0.85, 'task-1'),
31:     createMockRun('run-2', 'gpt-4o', '2026-04-05', 0.75, 'task-2'),
32:     createMockRun('run-3', 'claude-3', '2026-04-01', 0.90, 'task-1'),
33:     createMockRun('run-4', 'claude-3', '2026-04-10', 0.88, 'task-3'),
34:     createMockRun('run-5', 'gpt-4o', '2026-03-01', 0.70, 'task-1'),
35:   ];
36: 
37:   it('should filter by model', () => {
38:     const filters: QueryFilters = { model: 'gpt-4o' };
39:     const filtered = filterRuns(runs, filters);
40:     expect(filtered).toHaveLength(3);
41:     expect(filtered.every(r => r.model === 'gpt-4o')).toBe(true);
42:   });
43: 
44:   it('should filter by task', () => {
45:     const filters: QueryFilters = { task: 'task-1' };
46:     const filtered = filterRuns(runs, filters);
47:     expect(filtered).toHaveLength(3);
48:     expect(filtered.every(r => r.task_id === 'task-1')).toBe(true);
49:   });
50: 
51:   it('should filter by date range', () => {
52:     const filters: QueryFilters = {
53:       startDate: '2026-04-01',
54:       endDate: '2026-04-05',
55:     };
56:     const filtered = filterRuns(runs, filters);
57:     expect(filtered).toHaveLength(3);
58:   });
59: 
60:   it('should filter by score range', () => {
61:     const filters: QueryFilters = {
62:       minScore: 0.80,
63:       maxScore: 0.90,
64:     };
65:     const filtered = filterRuns(runs, filters);
66:     expect(filtered).toHaveLength(3);
67:     expect(filtered.every(r => r.total_score >= 0.80 && r.total_score <= 0.90)).toBe(true);
68:   });
69: 
70:   it('should combine multiple filters', () => {
71:     const filters: QueryFilters = {
72:       model: 'gpt-4o',
73:       minScore: 0.75,
74:     };
75:     const filtered = filterRuns(runs, filters);
76:     expect(filtered).toHaveLength(2);
77:     expect(filtered.every(r => r.model === 'gpt-4o' && r.total_score >= 0.75)).toBe(true);
78:   });
79: 
80:   it('should return all runs when no filters provided', () => {
81:     const filtered = filterRuns(runs, {});
82:     expect(filtered).toHaveLength(runs.length);
83:   });
84: });
85: 
86: describe('Summary Statistics', () => {
87:   it('should compute mean correctly', () => {
88:     const scores = [0.80, 0.85, 0.90, 0.75];
89:     const stats = computeStats(scores);
90:     expect(stats.mean).toBeCloseTo(0.825, 3);
91:   });
92: 
93:   it('should compute median correctly for odd count', () => {
94:     const scores = [0.80, 0.90, 0.70];
95:     const stats = computeStats(scores);
96:     expect(stats.median).toBeCloseTo(0.80, 3);
97:   });
98: 
99:   it('should compute median correctly for even count', () => {
100:     const scores = [0.80, 0.90, 0.70, 0.85];
101:     const stats = computeStats(scores);
102:     expect(stats.median).toBeCloseTo(0.825, 3);
103:   });
104: 
105:   it('should compute p95 correctly', () => {
106:     const scores = Array.from({ length: 100 }, (_, i) => i / 100);
107:     const stats = computeStats(scores);
108:     expect(stats.p95).toBeCloseTo(0.94, 2);
109:   });
110: 
111:   it('should handle single score', () => {
112:     const scores = [0.85];
113:     const stats = computeStats(scores);
114:     expect(stats.mean).toBe(stats.median);
115:     expect(stats.p95).toBe(0.85);
116:   });
117: });
118: 
119: describe('CSV Export', () => {
120:   const runs: RunDetail[] = [
121:     createMockRun('run-1', 'gpt-4o', '2026-04-01', 0.85, 'task-1'),
122:     createMockRun('run-2', 'gpt-4o', '2026-04-05', 0.75, 'task-2'),
123:   ];
124: 
125:   it('should generate valid CSV with headers', () => {
126:     const csv = exportToCSV(runs);
127:     const lines = csv.split('\n');
128:     expect(lines[0]).toContain('id');
129:     expect(lines[0]).toContain('model');
130:     expect(lines[0]).toContain('total_score');
131:   });
132: 
133:   it('should include all run data rows', () => {
134:     const csv = exportToCSV(runs);
135:     const lines = csv.split('\n').filter(l => l.length > 0);
136:     expect(lines.length).toBe(runs.length + 1);
137:   });
138: 
139:   it('should escape CSV special characters', () => {
140:     const runsWithSpecial: RunDetail[] = [
141:       { ...createMockRun('run-1', 'gpt-4o', '2026-04-01', 0.85), model: 'gpt-4o, mini' },
142:     ];
143:     const csv = exportToCSV(runsWithSpecial);
144:     expect(csv).toContain('"gpt-4o, mini"');
145:   });
146: 
147:   it('should handle empty array', () => {
148:     const csv = exportToCSV([]);
149:     const lines = csv.split('\n').filter(l => l.length > 0);
150:     expect(lines.length).toBe(1);
151:     expect(lines[0]).toContain('id');
152:   });
153: });

(End of file - total 153 lines)
</content>

part.text:
Done. Here's a summary of the changes:

**3 files modified:**

1. **`src/lib/export.ts`** — Added `datasetVersion?: string` to `QueryFilters` interface (line 6) and added filtering logic in `filterRuns` (lines 27-29) that compares `filters.datasetVersion` against `run.dataset_version`.

2. **`app/api/runs/route.ts`** — Added parsing of the `datasetVersion` query parameter (line 18).

3. **`tests/lib/export.test.ts`** — Updated `createMockRun` to accept `datasetVersion`, added `dataset_version` values to existing test data, and added two new tests:
   - `should filter by datasetVersion` — verifies standalone filtering
   - `should combine datasetVersion with model filter` — verifies interaction with the existing `model` filter

**All 417 tests pass** across 34 test files (including the 2 new tests).
